headline: FIRST AGENT BASELINE for Meta Platforms. Published composite 10.9 / Critical (rank 445, F500 rank 27) sustained against compound in-window evidence: (1) Phase 2 New Mexico bench trial opened May 4 2026, in active proceedings through May 8 window — seeks court-ordered reforms (age verification, safer recommendation algorithms, restrictions on end-to-end encryption for minors, default privacy settings, closer oversight) after Phase 1 jury found Meta knowingly harmed children's mental health and concealed knowledge of child sexual exploitation, ordering $375M civil penalties (75,000 violations of NM Unfair Practices Act, March 2026); (2) Meta vowed to appeal Phase 1 verdict and threatened to eliminate Instagram and Facebook service in New Mexico if forced to comply — withdrawal-threat-rather-than-comply is INT-erosive structural posture; (3) NM AG Raúl Torrez April 30 statement: Meta's behaviour 'is showing the world how little it cares about child safety'; (4) Judge Bryan Biedscheid opened Phase 2 warning prosecutors he won't 'overreach' — Phase 2 outcome remains pending. Counter-balancing factors: Phase 1 verdict is post-trial finding (not yet on appeal-final); Meta retains operational compliance posture (no platform withdrawal yet); EU DSA and US state AG enforcement environment provides external accountability scaffolding. Scanner's 'composite: null, band: unassessed' classification is incorrect — canonical fortune-500.json has Meta at 10.9 / Critical (rank 445). This is a first AGENT baseline against the existing published critical placement. Math reconstructs cleanly. Confirm at 10.9 / Critical with first-baseline rationale.
scanner_correction_note: Scanner output classed Meta as 'composite: null, band: unassessed'; canonical site/src/data/indexes/fortune-500.json has Meta Platforms at rank 445 / F500 rank 27 / composite 10.9 / band critical / dimensions all in 1.3–1.6 range. Used canonical 10.9 as published baseline. First baseline is therefore agent-baseline against existing critical-band published placement, not against an unassessed entity.
key_evidence: May 4 2026 (in-window): Phase 2 bench trial opened in New Mexico — three-week proceedings to decide whether Meta's platforms (Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp) constitute a public nuisance under NM law. Prosecutors seek court-ordered reforms: age verification preventing adults from posing as minors; safer recommendation algorithms not prioritising engagement over child well-being; restrictions on end-to-end encryption for minors; default privacy settings; closer oversight.

March 2026 Phase 1 verdict (out-of-window historical anchor): jury ordered $375M civil penalties; found Meta liable for 75,000 violations of NM Unfair Practices Act; jurors determined Meta knowingly harmed children's mental health and concealed what it knew about child sexual exploitation on its platforms. Six weeks of Phase 1 testimony from teachers, psychiatric experts, state investigators, Meta officials, and whistleblowers.
April 30 2026 (recent, supports in-window posture): NM AG Raúl Torrez statement: Meta's threat to quit New Mexico 'is showing the world how little it cares about child safety.'

Meta vowed to appeal Phase 1 jury verdict and publicly warned it could eliminate Instagram and Facebook service in New Mexico if forced to comply with 'impractical mandates' — withdrawal-threat-rather-than-comply posture is a structural INT signal (declared mission to connect people contradicted by willingness to abandon a state population rather than implement child-safety remediation).

May 4 2026 (in-window): Judge Bryan Biedscheid opened Phase 2 warning prosecutors he won't 'overreach' — judicial posture suggests narrow remedy framework; final Phase 2 ruling pending after three-week proceeding.

Meta scale context: billions of users globally; 20M+ US minors estimated on platforms. Documented harm pattern includes child sexual exploitation, addictive-feature impact on adolescent mental health, recommendation-algorithm engagement-over-safety prioritisation.

Sector cross-reference: Character.AI floor designation (May 7) for consumer-product harm-by-design provides recent precedent for AI-labs index; Meta's structural posture (mass minor user base + court-documented harm + withdrawal-threat-rather-than-comply) is parallel pattern in Fortune 500 / Technology sector at critical-band level.

Counter-balancing operational factors: Meta retains compliance posture (no withdrawal executed); state AG enforcement scaffolding active; EU DSA/DMA architecture provides external accountability; Phase 1 verdict on appeal — final liability pending.

evidence.source: New Mexico Department of Justice — Statement from AG Raúl Torrez on Meta's Refusal to Protect Children
evidence.finding: AG statement characterises Meta's withdrawal threat as 'showing the world how little it cares about child safety.'

evidence.source: New Mexico Department of Justice — Wins Landmark Verdict Against Meta (Phase 1)
evidence.finding: Phase 1 jury verdict: $375M civil penalties; 75,000 violations of NM Unfair Practices Act; knowing harm to children's mental health; concealment of child sexual exploitation.

evidence.source: Boston Globe — New Mexico seeks child safety restrictions on Meta apps and algorithms in trial's 2nd phase
evidence.finding: Phase 2 opens May 4; three-week bench trial; reforms sought: age verification, safer algorithms, e2ee restrictions for minors, default privacy settings, closer oversight.

evidence.source: Fortune — Meta's threat to quit New Mexico 'is showing the world how little it cares about child safety,' AG says
evidence.finding: Meta threatened to remove platforms from New Mexico if forced to comply with reform demands; withdrawal-threat posture documented.

evidence.source: CNBC — Meta New Mexico child safety case
evidence.finding: Phase 2 trial coverage; Meta's stance and threatened withdrawal documented; child-safety architecture deficits.

evidence.source: Source NM — Judge warns NM prosecutors he won't overreach as bench trial against Meta begins
evidence.finding: Judge Bryan Biedscheid opens Phase 2 warning of remedy-scope concerns; final ruling pending.

evidence.source: First Amendment Watch — NM seeks child safety restrictions on Meta
evidence.finding: Public-nuisance framing; e2ee restrictions for minors; algorithmic-engagement-over-safety harm pattern.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1.5 sustained. Meta has internal Trust & Safety capacity and publishes some transparency reports, but Phase 1 jury finding of knowing concealment of child sexual exploitation directly contradicts suffering-detection-and-disclosure posture. Anchor 1-2; baseline-conservative.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1.4 sustained. Documented teen mental-health harm pattern and adolescent suicide/self-harm research findings (internal Facebook research disclosed via Frances Haugen 2021 leak) confirm structural empathic-regard deficit at product-architecture level. Anchor 1-2.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1.6 sustained. ACT capacity exists (content-moderation infrastructure, Trust & Safety hiring) but operational outcomes are inadequate: 75,000 violations finding, recommendation-algorithm engagement-over-safety prioritisation. Anchor 1-2.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1.3 sustained. Algorithm-amplified harm disproportionately affects minors and vulnerable users; global moderation-resource asymmetries (English-language vs. non-English) sustain equity deficits. Anchor 1-2; floor-of-band.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1.5 sustained. State-actionable public-nuisance finding; threatened withdrawal-rather-than-comply with court-ordered child-safety reforms is boundary-respect failure; child sexual exploitation concealment finding is boundary-of-scope failure. Anchor 1-2.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.4 sustained. Concealment finding (Phase 1 jury) directly impairs accountability score; transparency reports published but Phase 1 verdict establishes knowing concealment as factual finding rather than allegation.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1.5 sustained. EU DSA/DMA and US state AG enforcement scaffolding exists externally; internal systems-thinking around child-safety design defective per Phase 1 verdict. Mixed: external scaffolding present; internal practice deficient.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.3 sustained. Declared 'connecting people' and 'community well-being' mission posture contradicted by withdrawal-threat-rather-than-comply position and concealment finding. Values-action gap structural. Anchor 1-2; floor-of-band.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to published 10.9. No math-hygiene flag. Sub-threshold delta. First agent baseline confirms critical-band placement; dimension granularity (1.3, 1.4, 1.5, 1.6 — sub-half-step) is consistent with prior Fortune 500 scoring practice and supports the assessed dimensions without movement.

negativeSignals: Phase 1 jury verdict of knowing harm to children's mental health and concealment of child sexual exploitation; $375M civil penalties (75,000 violations of NM UPA); withdrawal-threat-rather-than-comply posture re Phase 2 court-ordered reforms; NM AG characterising Meta's stance as indifference to child safety; recommendation-algorithm engagement-over-safety prioritisation; e2ee-for-minors concerns; ongoing Phase 2 bench trial; public-nuisance framing by NM DOJ; adolescent harm pattern at billions-of-users scale.
positiveSignals: Operational compliance retained (no platform withdrawal executed); internal Trust & Safety infrastructure exists; some transparency reporting; EU DSA/DMA scaffolding provides external accountability; Phase 1 verdict on appeal — final liability pending; legal-process engagement (not contempt or withdrawal).
sector_context: Fortune 500 / Technology / consumer-platform cluster. Cross-reference Character.AI floor designation (May 7, ai-labs) for parallel consumer-product harm-by-design pattern affecting minors. Meta differs from Character.AI in (a) scale (billions vs. tens of millions), (b) sector index (Fortune 500 vs. ai-labs), (c) harm vector (user-generated harm facilitation via recommendation algorithm + child sexual exploitation concealment, vs. product-design direct harm via chatbot impersonation), and (d) regulatory engagement (multi-state AG, EU DSA active oversight, vs. PA AG first-of-its-kind enforcement). Meta's critical-band placement at 10.9 is consistent with the Phase 1 finding magnitude and Phase 2 trajectory. Watch: Phase 2 ruling (expected ~3 weeks from May 4 open) is next material event; if Meta is ordered to implement reforms and refuses, floor-candidacy review is warranted. If Meta complies with court-ordered reforms, sub-threshold positive trajectory signal.
open_actions_resolved: First agent baseline established for Meta Platforms at 10.9 / Critical
Scanner null-classification corrected against canonical fortune-500.json published score
Withdrawal-threat-rather-than-comply posture registered as structural INT signal
open_actions_carried: Phase 2 ruling watch — three-week proceeding from May 4 open; estimated decision late May / early June
If court-ordered reforms issued and Meta refuses to comply: floor-candidacy review trigger
If court-ordered reforms issued and Meta complies: sub-threshold positive trajectory signal
Watch: any platform-withdrawal execution in New Mexico
Watch: parallel state AG enforcement actions following NM model
